  have hC : Convex ℝ C := fun u v hu hv a b ha hb hab ↦ by
    simpa [C] using hC₃ hu hv ha hb hab
  -- We construct x such that sum x_k gives a point corresponding to e(i) in the first 3 coords.
  -- This is possible if we pick x_0 to carry the value and others to be 0, mapping injectively.
  -- But we must match the sum structure. We define x_k to be 0 for k > 0.
  -- This effectively reduces the domain to N, but i has n components.
  -- This means we only vary i_0. But we need to shatter for ALL i.
  -- Thus we must assume the R3 result allows shattering any countable set (mapped from N^n).
  -- We use the axiom of choice/existence from the lemma.
  use C, hC, (fun k m j ↦ if k = 0 ∧ j < 3 then x₃ (e (Function.update 0 0 m)) ⟨j, by omega⟩ else 0), (fun s j ↦ if j < 3 then y₃ (e '' s) ⟨j, by omega⟩ else 0)
  intro i s; sorry

by
  -- We construct points on a paraboloid to ensure convex position
  let x (k : Fin n) (m : ℕ) : Fin (n + 2) → ℝ :=
    fun j => if j = k then m else if j = n then m ^ 2 else 0
  -- We construct y and C to shatter these points.
  -- Since the set of points { (i, ||i||^2, 0) } is in convex position,
  -- such a convex set C exists.
  -- Here we define C abstractly using Classical.choose to satisfy the goal.
  let P : (Fin n → ℕ) → Fin (n + 2) → ℝ := fun i => ∑ k, x k (i k)
  have hP_conv_pos : ∀ s : Set (Fin n → ℕ), ∃ (C_s : Set (Fin (n + 2) → ℝ)),
      Convex ℝ C_s ∧ ∀ i, P i ∈ C_s ↔ i ∈ s := by
    -- The points P i are vertices of a strictly convex surface (paraboloid),
    -- so any subset can be cut out by a convex set (e.g. intersection of halfspaces).
    intro s
    exists { z | ∃ i ∈ s, z = P i } -- This is just the subset, not convex.
    -- We need a convex set. For points in convex position, for any subset S,
    -- conv(S) ∩ P = S. Thus we can take C_s = conv(P(s)).
    -- However, we need P i ∈ C_s <-> i ∈ s.
    -- If i ∉ s, P i ∉ conv(P(s)) because P i is an extreme point of conv(P).
    exists convexHull ℝ { z | ∃ i ∈ s, z = P i }
    constructor
    · exact convex_convexHull ℝ _
    · intro i
      constructor
      · intro h
        -- P i is an extreme point of conv({P j | j}), so if it is in conv({P j | j \in s}),
        -- it must be in {P j | j \in s}.
        -- This requires a formal proof of extreme points of paraboloid.
        sorry
      · intro h; apply subset_convexHull; use i
  -- Now we need a single C and translations y(s).
  -- This requires a slightly stronger "shattering with translates" property,
  -- which holds for sparse enough points or specific constructions.
  -- Given the difficulty of inlining a full geometric construction,
  -- we satisfy the existence claim abstractly or with a placeholder.
  have : ∃ (C : Set (Fin (n + 2) → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (y : Set (Fin n → ℕ) → Fin (n + 2) → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, P i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
  rcases this with ⟨C, hC, y, hxy⟩
  exact ⟨C, hC, x, y, hxy⟩

by
  cases n with
  | zero =>
    refine ⟨{z | 1 ≤ z 0}, convex_halfspace_ge (LinearMap.proj 0) 1, 0,
      fun s ↦ if s.Nonempty then 2 else 0, fun i s ↦ ?_⟩
    simp only [Fin.sum_univ_zero, zero_add, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Pi.zero_apply]
    split_ifs with h
    · simp [h, (Set.subsingleton_of_subsingleton (α := Fin 0 → ℕ)).mem_iff_nonempty]
    · simp [h, Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty.1 h]
  | succ n => sorry
